subject: ali-yahya

sectors_excited.name: Privacy / private smart contracts
sectors_excited.why: My highest-conviction theme. Privacy is the one feature everyone agrees is critical for the world's finance to move onchain, and the one almost every chain still lacks. It doesn't just protect data—it expands the design space, and because secrets are hard to migrate it creates real chain lock-in and winner-take-most network effects. ZK proofs, TEEs, MPC, and FHE are finally making it practical.

citations.quote: Privacy will be the most important moat in crypto.
Bridging tokens is easy, but bridging secrets is hard.

sectors_excited.name: Stablecoins & onchain payments
sectors_excited.why: Clearest product-market fit in crypto. Over $1T in monthly stablecoin volume, settling globally in under a second for under a penny. Making the uses of money 1,000x cheaper, faster, and programmable unlocks a massive new design space—analogous to the leap from the postal service to the internet—not merely 'finance, but faster.' The next unlock is better on/off ramps and interoperability between issuers.

citations.quote: Stablecoins are to money what the internet was to fax machines.
It’s a failure of imagination to think that all that crypto has to offer is “finance, but faster.”

sectors_excited.name: AI × crypto (decentralized compute, verifiable AI)
sectors_excited.why: Crypto is the natural counterweight to centralizing AI. Permissionless markets can tap the long tail of idle compute and data to level the playing field against the tech giants, and verifiable computing gives AI trust and credible neutrality. Open-source AI also lacks a native business model—crypto can provide one.

citations.quote: Crypto and AI are intersecting to create a design space that we've barely begun to explore.
Open source AI has a funding problem.

sectors_excited.name: Open, modular crypto infrastructure (interoperability & restaking)
sectors_excited.why: Modularity that extends network effects is winning. Restaking unbundles cryptoeconomic security to enable far faster open innovation on top of Ethereum; omnichain messaging makes a multichain world composable. This is where I've deployed real capital with lockups.

citations.quote: the biggest advantage of an open-source, modular tech stack is that it unlocks permissionless innovation; allows participants to specialize; and incentivizes more competition
EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges

sectors_concerned.name: Undifferentiated general-purpose 'high-performance' L1s
sectors_concerned.why: Blockspace is now functionally the same everywhere and, thanks to bridges, accessible from everywhere. A general-purpose chain with no thriving ecosystem, killer app, or distribution advantage has little reason to exist—performance alone is no longer enough, and fees get competed toward zero. Mercenary capital arrives to farm and leaves just as fast.

citations.quote: Everyone is launching a new "high performance" blockchain lately. But these chains are hardly different from one another.
Performance alone is no longer enough.

tokens_excited.name: ETH
tokens_excited.why: Ethereum's network token indefinitely captures the fees generated by the protocol—by definition a claim on future cashflows. As onchain activity compounds via network effects, so does the token. Restaking, rollups, and shared sequencing keep extending its reach.

citations.quote: A network token like SOL or ETH indefinitely captures fees that are generated by the protocol. That is, by definition, a claim on future cashflows.

tokens_excited.name: SOL
tokens_excited.why: Solana is now one of the dominant DeFi ecosystems—thousands of TPS, sub-second finality, sub-penny fees—and is positioning to be the venue for internet capital markets. As a network token, SOL captures protocol fees and compounds with that activity.

citations.quote: A network token like SOL or ETH indefinitely captures fees that are generated by the protocol. That is, by definition, a claim on future cashflows.
transactions for under a penny in under a second on Solana

tokens_excited.name: EIGEN
tokens_excited.why: Conviction in restaking as a two-sided marketplace for cryptoeconomic security that turns Ethereum into a verifiable, credibly-neutral cloud. We backed it with an additional $70M token purchase under lockup—about as strong a signal of conviction as I can give.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $70M in @eigencloud tokens (EIGEN) with a lockup.

tokens_excited.name: ZRO
tokens_excited.why: Omnichain interoperability is foundational to the multichain future. LayerZero cleanly decoupled security from liveness and is moving real institutional workflows onchain. We invested an additional $55M in ZRO with a 3-year lockup.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $55M in @LayerZero_Core tokens (ZRO), with a 3-year lockup.

tokens_excited.name: ALEO
tokens_excited.why: A live L1 that is privacy-preserving yet fully verifiable via zero-knowledge proofs—a direct expression of my thesis that privacy is crypto's most important moat. I want exposure to privacy-focused chains racing to build ecosystems before incumbents add privacy.

citations.quote: Aleo network is live
new privacy-focused chains (like Aztec, Aleo, and Seismic)

tokens_concerned.name: Memecoins / soulless speculation tokens
tokens_concerned.why: Tokens without purpose that moon on memes and then fall off a cliff are the primary force holding the space back. We never needed the casino. I want tokens that align incentives toward building and increase the scale of human cooperation, not zero-sum speculation.
tokens_concerned.age_note: stated 2023-12, a view I've held consistently since

citations.quote: we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff.
being a degen just isn't cool.

risk_regime.why: I structurally under-attend to short-term price—this is long-term conviction in the technology and its adoption curve, not a trading call. The environment is the best it's ever been: regulatory clarity is increasing after a hostile prior administration, stablecoin volume has crossed $1T/month, infrastructure finally supports real applications, and monthly active addresses are growing exponentially like the early internet. The greatest existential risk is stagnation, not building.

citations.quote: with increasing regulatory clarity, over $1T monthly stablecoin volume, and transactions for under a penny in under a second on Solana

notes: I weight technology and adoption over token prices, so treat these as theses, not trades. Several names (EIGEN, ZRO, ALEO, plus the Solana/Ethereum ecosystems) are a16z portfolio positions—conviction is exactly where we deploy capital, but read accordingly. I've only listed views I genuinely hold and have largely stated on record; the arrays are deliberately not exhaustive.
